carbon_policy_signal.item: EU doubles down on carbon tax for international flights; extends ETS scope and signals tightening carbon pricing ambition.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Aviation carbon-tax expansion broadens ETS footprint; signals EU commitment to carbon pricing depth; modest uplift to power via extended fuel-switch logic if aviation fuel constraints ripple into crude-to-power fuel arb.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (140 days old, as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark stale (135 days old, as of 2025-12-31) — merit-order and fuel-switch transparency degraded; rely on GB Power (93rd-percentile), Clean Spark (92nd-percentile), and storage deficit (13 pp below seasonal) for current regime signal.
risk_flags: Storage at 35.85% (13 pp below seasonal) — refill pace will set July–September thermal headroom and power curve shape.

GB Power 93rd-percentile (127.17 EUR/MWh) and Clean Spark 92nd-percentile (39.29 EUR/MWh) suggest supply-constrained or weather-driven tightness; coal data stale (140 days old) — merit order transparency gap.
Iran war and shadow-fleet sanctions create Brent upside tail; higher fuel oil / LNG substitution could extend thermal premium into Q3.
Trump anti-renewables rhetoric and political headwinds in Europe may delay wind build-out, supporting forward (Cal+1, Cal+2) power prices.

base.summary: Storage deficit sustains thermal dispatch; renewable policy acceleration gradual; Brent stable near $80–82/bbl.

downside.summary: Storage refill accelerates; Portugal policy enacted early, renewables oversupply grid; demand destruction offsets geopolitical premium.

upside.summary: Shadow-fleet sanctions impact Russian Urals flows; Brent spikes to $85+, tightening LNG arb and fuel-oil substitution.

themes: Extreme GB and German power valuations; thermal in the money
Storage deficit pressures H2 refill; geopolitical risk premium embedded
Policy pivot to renewables vs. near-term fuel-switch regime
Shadow-fleet sanctions tighten Russian crude; Brent upside risk
top_drivers: Storage 13 pp below seasonal + Iran war policy pivot to renewables = tight gas regime but forward-looking demand destruction; near-term thermal call sustained by supply gap.
GB Power 93rd-percentile and Clean Spark 92nd-percentile reflect combined effect of storage deficit, potential weather tightness, and fuel-switch premium on high-carbon dispatch.
Shadow-fleet sanctions and Ukraine sea-drone escalation elevate Brent risk; higher crude lifts LNG arb and fuel-oil substitution, supporting EU thermal generation costs.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 93rd-percentile and Clean Spark at 92nd-percentile signal fuel-switch regime; storage 13 pp below seasonal average underpins thermal call despite Iran-war policy acceleration toward renewables.

watchlist.why: Russian Urals export volume cuts lift Brent, triggering fuel-oil and LNG arb into EU; net bullish thermal and power generation cost.
At 13 pp deficit vs. seasonal norm; slow refill could force sustained gas demand destruction and/or extended thermal dispatch into summer.

watchlist.metric: Portugal renewable auction schedule & fossil-fuel reduction legislation
watchlist.why: Iran-war policy acceleration; timing and capacity additions will signal Iberian grid decarbonization pace and impact on regional power prices.

watchlist.metric: Brent / TTF arb and LNG flow reversals
watchlist.why: Shadow-fleet sanctions and Hormuz risk elevate crude; if Brent breaks above $85/bbl, LNG arb narrows and EU gas demand faces downside pressure.
Aviation carbon-tax expansion signals ETS ambition; fuel-switch logic and industrial compliance timing could trigger repricing in H2 2026.

watchlist_dated.name: Iran escalation updates / Hormuz transit risk
watchlist_dated.why: Geopolitical flashpoint; direct LNG route constraint could spike Brent and TTF arb, forcing EU thermal premium higher.
